title: Transitive coverage with type-exclude and container indirection
description:
Make `label-coverage` traverse the container transitive subtree by default, add a `child-type-exclude` option, and let a coverage rule resolve its criteria-bearing container from a `brackets:` label.

## Background

Coverage walks direct dependencies only; under transitive reduction a container links only to sink children, so a criterion satisfied deeper reads as uncovered. The per-issue rules must keep their same-issue semantics.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `label-coverage` credits a satisfying issue anywhere in the transitive subtree; `criteria-to-check` and `criteria-label-match` are unchanged.
- [ ] `child-type-exclude` drops listed types from coverage candidates and halts the walk through them.
- [ ] A `label-coverage` rule matching the breakdown node resolves the container via its `brackets:` label and evaluates that container criteria.
